channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What the private sector can teach government about cloud computing
item.description: Cloud computing has been at the forefront of government IT strategies for many years. When cloud computing first became a thing in 2008, the government led the way, including NIST creating a definition of cloud computing. I worked closely with them then and watched the whole thing play out.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Here are crypto’s biggest heists after Bybit’s $1.5 billion hack
item.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit said last week hackers had stolen digital tokens worth around $1.5 billion, in what researchers called the biggest crypto heist of all time.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft cancels data center lease plans, raising questions
item.description: Microsoft is slowing down or possibly redirecting its data center construction plans, according to a report by TD Cowen. Analysts with the brokerage say its a sign of the company “potentially being in an oversupply position.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies
item.description: VA officials are working on an updated EHR deployment schedule beyond the Michigan go-lives in mid-2026. The post VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SEC ends investigation into Robinhood's crypto arm
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has closed its investigation into the cryptocurrency arm of Robinhood without taking action against the online brokerage, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Santorini's earthquake swarm is declining gradually as thousands return to the Greek island
item.description: Scientists from the University of Athens monitoring an earthquake swarm near the island of Santorini say the phenomenon is in “gradual decline” nearly a month after the multiple tremors started.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft gives Azure AI users a place to experiment with latest technologies
item.description: With the pace of innovation in AI accelerating, it’s important for developers to get early access to new tools and technologies. To that end, Microsoft has introduced a module inside its Azure AI Foundry service that aims to bring the latest innovations and research in AI to developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple planning to invest $500B in US, hire 20K people
item.description: Apple announced Monday that it plans to spend more than $500 billion in the United States and hire more than 20,000 people over the next several years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: This is what no one planned could happen after taking a DNA test
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says DNA tests pose privacy risks like shared genetic information, inaccuracies and hard data deletion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple pledges to spend $500 billon in the US over the next few years
item.description: Apple will invest the amount of revenue it makes in an entire year by 2029 to support US manufacturing and R&D.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: More details of Samsung's tri-fold phone have leaked – and it could launch alongside the Galaxy Z Fold 7
item.description: A new report from South Korea gives us a better idea of the phone's dimensions and when it might be unveiled.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GenAI’s unexpected impact: Disrupting high-skilled tech jobs, too
item.description: A new Brookings Institution report on generative AI (genAI) found that the more highly skilled a tech worker is, the more vulnerable they are to having their jobs supplemented by the technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 machine learning mistakes and how to avoid them
item.description: Machine learning technology is taking hold across many sectors as its applications are more widely adopted. The research firm Fortune Business Insights forecasts the global machine-learning market will expand from $26.03 billion in 2023 to $225.91 billion by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why a proposed ban on lab-grown meat is getting pushback from Nebraska ranchers
item.description: The prospect of banning the sale of so-called lab-grown meat might seem like a no-brainer in Nebraska, where beef is king, but some of the proposal’s staunchest opposition has come from ranchers and farming groups who say they can compete without the government’s help. Nebraska Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Silicon Valley Auto Show
item.description: Visitors of all ages explored the latest vehicles from 17 different manufacturers, including Toyota, Kia, Porsche, Lexus and Infiniti.
item.dc:creator: Ray Chavez, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla recalling more than 375,000 vehicles due to power steering issue
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said that the recall is for certain 2023 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Why enterprises fail at finops
item.description: As cloud spending continues to climb, the discipline known as finops (financial operations) has emerged as a critical strategy for managing and optimizing cloud investments. This is no longer news. Finops has been a discipline and a toolkit for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Deno update brings OpenTelemetry integration
item.description: Deno Land has released Deno 2.2, a new version of the Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript that features built-in OpenTelemetry for monitoring logs, metrics, and traces. The new Deno release also features Linter updates that include a new plugin system and new rules for React and Preact users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Huge data leak exposes 14 million customer shipping records
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Hipshipper, which is used by eBay, Shopify and Amazon sellers, exposed millions of shipping labels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
